hero.title: Capture any page. Send to any chat.
hero.subtitle: Clip the current page into structured fields and deliver it with your saved prompt to an IM or AI Agent chat session.
hero.payloadPreview.label: Structured payload preview
useCases.section.title: Use cases
useCases.section.intro: Use the same capture-to-chat path for solo research, team coordination, and agent-driven knowledge loops.
useCases.personalKnowledge.title: Personal knowledge capture
useCases.personalKnowledge.description: Save a web page with its structured fields and your own prompt before it gets lost in tabs.
useCases.personalKnowledge.evidence: Built for local-first clipping into repeatable knowledge workflows.
useCases.teamSharing.title: Team sharing
useCases.teamSharing.description: Send source-backed page context into a shared chat session without manual copy-paste cleanup.
useCases.teamSharing.evidence: Keeps title, URL, description, content, and prompt together.
useCases.agentWorkflows.title: Agent / llm-wiki workflows
useCases.agentWorkflows.description: Route structured page context into AI Agent sessions that turn browsing into a durable knowledge stream.
useCases.agentWorkflows.evidence: Designed around the original llm-wiki capture-to-chat loop.
payload.title: Structured-payload example
payload.description: Show the same six fields web2chat captures before it opens the target chat tab.
payload.fields.title: title
payload.values.title: MDN Web Docs — ClipboardEvent
payload.values.description: Clipboard events expose paste and copy flows that web apps can intercept for rich text editors.
payload.values.content: ClipboardEvent lets the extension deliver structured page context into web chat editors through direct browser interaction instead of a remote server relay.
payload.values.prompt: Summarize the page, extract key actions, and file it into our shared knowledge thread.
supportedPlatforms.title: Supported platforms
supportedPlatforms.intro: The current shipped scope stays limited to four platforms and keeps known risks explicit.
supportedPlatforms.openclaw.label: OpenClaw
supportedPlatforms.discord.label: Discord
supportedPlatforms.discord.detail: Channel delivery in the current shipped scope
supportedPlatforms.slack.label: Slack
supportedPlatforms.slack.detail: Channel delivery in the current shipped scope
supportedPlatforms.telegram.label: Telegram
supportedPlatforms.telegram.detail: Shipped platform with current adapter support
flow.section.title: Three-step core flow
flow.section.intro: The product stays focused on one reliable path from page capture to chat delivery.
flow.capture.title: Capture page
flow.capture.description: Click the extension and collect the current page into structured metadata.
flow.chooseTarget.title: Choose target
flow.chooseTarget.description: Pick the IM or AI Agent session URL and keep the paired prompt ready.
flow.sendToChat.title: Send to chat
flow.sendToChat.description: Open the target tab and deliver the structured payload through direct browser interaction.
trust.section.title: Privacy / permissions trust
trust.section.intro: Public claims stay grounded in the extension's actual privacy model and production permission shape.
trust.privacy.title: Privacy facts
trust.privacy.facts.userTriggeredCapture: User-triggered capture only — web2chat processes data only when you click the extension icon.
trust.privacy.facts.localStorage: Local-first storage — captured data stays in browser storage on your device.
trust.privacy.facts.directBrowserDeliver: Direct browser delivery — dispatch happens through target tabs, not a relay service.
trust.privacy.facts.noRemoteServer: No remote server — web2chat does not operate or communicate with any remote backend.
trust.privacy.facts.noTelemetry: No telemetry — the extension does not ship usage analytics or tracking signals.
trust.privacy.facts.noThirdPartyAnalytic: No third-party analytics — no external analytics SDK is included.
trust.permissions.title: Permission facts
trust.permissions.facts.productionPermis: Production permissions: activeTab, alarms, scripting, storage, webNavigation.
trust.permissions.facts.productionHosts: Production host permissions cover Discord, Slack, and Telegram web origins only.
trust.permissions.facts.optionalOriginGr: OpenClaw and other user-deployed targets use an optional origin grant instead of static broad production host access.
knownLimits.title: Known limits
knownLimits.intro: Current closeout gaps stay documented here instead of being promoted as shipped proof.
knownLimits.telegram: Telegram is shipped, but live session UAT is still pending and remains a known risk.
knownLimits.feishuLark: Feishu/Lark was evaluated and dropped from the shipped scope because shared URL targeting was not reliable enough.
knownLimits.nyquist: Phase 11/12 Nyquist closeout is still partial and tracked only as a known risk.
proof.label: mockup
proof.status: marketing demo aligned to current UI contract
